title: cassytha filiformis subsp. guineensis
intro: Cassytha filiformis subsp. guineensis, also known as the devil's twine or Cassytha, is a parasitic plant species that derives its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants. Traditionally, Cassytha has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Cassytha include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a treatment for wounds and snake bites. Cassytha can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for medicinal purposes, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies in West Africa.
